title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I have {{c1::two possible explanations}}(两种可能的解释). {{c2::The more whimsical explanation}}(更奇特的解释) is that maybe RL training makes the models {{c3::a little too single-minded}}(有点过于执着) and narrowly focused, {{c4::a little bit too unaware}}(有点过于无知), even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
original: I have two possible explanations. The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
translation: 我有两种可能的解释。更有趣的解释是，也许强化学习（RL）训练让模型变得过于专注，过于狭隘，在某种程度上甚至让它们变得不那么敏感，尽管它也让它们在其他方面变得更加敏感。
note:
phrases: two possible explanations | The more whimsical explanation | a little too single-minded | a little bit too unaware
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0007_c84335
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: abstraction_bridge | stance_positioning
transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.
expression_transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: An example would be, {{c1::let’s say}}(假设) you {{c2::use vibe coding}}(使用振动编码) to do something. You go to some place and then you get a bug.
original: An example would be, let’s say you use vibe coding to do something. You go to some place and then you get a bug.
translation: 比如，假设你用振动编码（vibe coding）做某件事。你去了某个地方，然后遇到了一个bug。
note:
phrases: let’s say | use vibe coding
title: Ilya-Sutskever

chunk: chunk_0006_13b210
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: discourse_organizer | strong_collocation
transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.
expression_transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: The idea that {{c1::we’d be investing}}(我们会投资) 1% of GDP in AI, {{c2::I feel like}}(我觉得) it would have felt like {{c3::a bigger deal}}(更大的影响), whereas right now it just feels... Ilya Sutskever
original: The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, I feel like it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels... Ilya Sutskever
translation: 我们投资1%的GDP用于AI的想法，我感觉这曾经会被认为是一件大事，而如今却只是觉得... 伊利亚·苏茨克弗
note:
phrases: we’d be investing | I feel like | a bigger deal
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: They are {{c1::doing so well}}(做得这么好). But the {{c2::economic impact}}(经济影响) seems to be {{c3::dramatically behind}}(远远落后).
original: They are doing so well. But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.
translation: 它们表现得非常好。但经济影响似乎远远落后于预期。
note:
phrases: doing so well | economic impact | dramatically behind
title: Ilya-Sutskever

chunk: chunk_0005_f794b9

target_lang: zh
phrase_types: strong_collocation | abstraction_bridge

expression_transfer: Useful for producing native-like collocations in formal discussion.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: It’s very difficult to {{c1::make sense of}}(理解) how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
original: It’s very difficult to make sense of how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 很难理解模型如何能在一方面做出令人惊叹的事情，另一方面却在某些情况下重复自己两次。
note:
phrases: make sense of
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel Should we {{c1::actually begin here}}(实际上开始于)? I think this is an interesting discussion.
original: Dwarkesh Patel Should we actually begin here? I think this is an interesting discussion.
translation: Dwarkesh Patel 我们应该从这里开始吗？我认为这是一个有趣的讨论。
note:
phrases: actually begin here
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you {{c1::tell the model}}(告诉模型), “{{c2::Can you please}}(请问) {{c3::fix the bug}}(修复这个错误)?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me {{c4::go fix that}}(去修复那个).”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”
translation: 然后你告诉模型：“能不能请你修复这个bug？”模型说：“哦我的天，你太对了。我确实有个bug。让我去修复它。”
note:
phrases: tell the model | Can you please | fix the bug | go fix that
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: {{c1::Isn’t it straight}}(这不是很直接吗) out of science fiction? Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels.
original: Isn’t it straight out of science fiction? Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels.
translation: 这难道不是直接来自科幻小说吗？Dwarkesh Patel 另一件疯狂的事情是缓慢起步的过程感觉如此正常。
note:
phrases: Isn’t it straight

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever {{c1::Don’t you think}}(你不觉得吗) so? All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening.
original: Ilya Sutskever Don’t you think so? All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克维尔难道你不这么认为吗？所有这些AI的东西和所有这些硅谷的事，竟然真的发生了。
note:
phrases: Don’t you think

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever {{c1::We get used}}(我们习惯了) to things pretty fast, {{c2::it turns out}}(原来). But also it’s {{c3::kind of abstract}}(有点抽象).
original: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, it turns out. But also it’s kind of abstract.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克维尔我们很快就能习惯事情，结果发现是这样。但同时这也有点抽象。
note:
phrases: We get used | it turns out | kind of abstract

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: {{c1::Back when people}}(以前人们) were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you need all the data.
original: Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you need all the data.
translation: 在人们进行预训练时，训练数据的选择问题已经有了答案，因为答案就是所有数据。当你进行预训练时，你需要所有的数据。
note:
phrases: Back when people
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But there is another explanation. {{c1::Back when people}}(以前人们) were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.
original: But there is another explanation. Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.
translation: 但还有一种解释。在人们进行预训练时，训练数据的选择问题已经有了答案，因为答案就是所有数据。
note:
phrases: Back when people

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I think the {{c1::impact of AI}}(人工智能的影响) is going to be felt. AI is going to be diffused through the economy.
original: I think the impact of AI is going to be felt. AI is going to be diffused through the economy.
translation: 我认为人工智能的影响将会显现出来。人工智能将会在整个经济中得到普及。
note:
phrases: impact of AI
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0003_b07743
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: abstraction_bridge
transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.
expression_transfer: Useful for lifting specific examples into a broader claim.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: There’ll be very strong {{c1::economic forces for}}(经济力量) this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you {{c2::expect that impact}}(预期的影响)?
original: There’ll be very strong economic forces for this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
translation: 这背后会有非常强大的经济力量推动，我认为其影响将会非常显著。Dwarkesh Patel：您预计这种影响会在什么时候出现？
note:
phrases: economic forces for | expect that impact
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever — {{c1::We're moving}}(我们正在移动) from the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever You know what’s crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?
original: Ilya Sutskever — We're moving from the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever You know what’s crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?
translation: Ilya Sutskever：我们正从‘规模化时代’过渡到‘研究时代’——解释模型的锯齿状现象 Ilya Sutskever：你知道吗？Dwarkesh Patel：什么意思？
note:
phrases: We're moving

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But the {{c1::economic impact seems}}(经济影响似乎) to be {{c2::dramatically behind}}(远远落后). It’s very difficult to {{c3::make sense of}}(理解), how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, {{c4::repeat itself}}(重复自己) twice in some situation?
original: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 但经济影响似乎远远落后。很难理解，模型怎么可能在一方面做出这些令人惊叹的事情，另一方面却在某些情况下重复自己两次？
note:
phrases: economic impact seems | dramatically behind | make sense of | repeat itself
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re {{c1::so right again}}(所以你又对了),”
original: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re so right again,”
translation: 然后你告诉它，“你又出现了新的第二个问题”，它就会说，“哦我的天，我怎么会犯这种错？你又是对的。”
note:
phrases: so right again

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: How to reconcile the fact that they are {{c1::doing so well}}(做得这么好) on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c2::pretty hard evals}}(相当严格的评估).”
original: How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.”
translation: 如何调和它们在评估测试中表现如此出色这一事实？你看看评估测试，就会想，“这些评估测试看起来相当难。”
note:
phrases: doing so well | pretty hard evals

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c1::pretty hard evals}}(相当严格的评估).” They are {{c2::doing so well}}(做得这么好). But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.
original: You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.” They are doing so well. But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.
translation: 你看看评估测试，就会想，“这些评估测试看起来相当难。”它们表现得这么好。但经济影响似乎远远落后。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: There are so many degrees of freedom. There is such a {{c1::huge variety}}(种类繁多) of RL environments you could produce.
original: There are so many degrees of freedom. There is such a huge variety of RL environments you could produce.
translation: 自由度实在是太多了。强化学习环境的种类也多得难以想象。
note:
phrases: huge variety

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: How is that possible? I’m not sure, but it does suggest that something {{c1::strange is going}}(奇怪的事情正在发生) on.
original: How is that possible? I’m not sure, but it does suggest that something strange is going on.
translation: 这怎么可能呢？我不太清楚，但这确实暗示着有些奇怪的事情正在发生。
